context: Evaluates the implementation of a Tauri command that reports the vault's existence and lock state. The command must check disk for vault.json presence and inspect the in-memory application state for an active master key. Accurate reflection of all three states (no vault, locked, unlocked) is required.

checklist.name: VaultStatus type
checklist.description: Defines a `VaultStatus` struct (or equivalent) with both a boolean `exists` field and a boolean `locked` field, serializable to JSON via serde.

checklist.name: Disk existence check
checklist.description: Correctly checks whether `vault.json` exists on disk using `FsRepo::vault_exists()` or equivalent filesystem inspection to set the `exists` field.

checklist.name: Lock state from AppState
checklist.description: Correctly determines the `locked` state by inspecting the mutex-protected `master_key` field in `AppState`—locked when the key is `None`, unlocked when it holds a value.

checklist.name: Tauri command registration
checklist.description: The function is annotated with `#[tauri::command]` and accepts `tauri::State<'_, AppState>` as a parameter so it can be invoked from the frontend.

checklist.name: Correct state combinations
checklist.description: Returns `{ exists: false, locked: true }` when no vault is present; `{ exists: true, locked: true }` when vault exists but key is absent; `{ exists: true, locked: false }` when vault exists and key is held.
